Your Chat UI Just Got an AI Roommate

Your AI agent belongs in the chat you already built. CometChat's iOS UI Kit lets you turn your existing messaging experience into an AI conversation with streaming responses, chat history, and a fully customizable UI built in. Connect your agent, match your brand, and ship without building AI chat from scratch.

Compact Message Composer by CometChat

Compact Message Composer by CometChatEverything users expect from modern chat. Out of the box. [...] 📌

Hey Product Hunt!   
  
Amit from CometChat here 👋

Every time someone tells us they're adding an AI agent to their iOS app, the same thing happens. They think the hard part is the AI. It's not. The hard part is that building a decent chat UI eats weeks you didn't budget for. Message bubbles, streaming, history screens; none of it is hard, it's just a slog, and it's the slog nobody warns you about.

So we stopped watching people rebuild the same thing and put it in our iOS UI Kit.

You get the actual chat experience out of the box: streaming responses, suggested prompts, and conversation history so people can pick up an old chat instead of starting from zero every time. Wire up your agent, restyle it to match your app, ship.

results.title: One Tool — 100+ AI Agents (tutorial for beginners)
results.content: between them to get the best results. Chat LM comes with a built-in feature called root LM, which makes all the decisions for you. It analyzes your prompts and automatically selects the model that will perform the task the best. For example, it might stick with Chad GBT for general writing tasks, but switch to a model like Gemini for analyzing specific data. All the models here are fully packed with features. There is still multimodal, meaning they can analyze documents, work with images, generate new files and reports, dive into deep research on papers, and much more. For instance, you could upload a PDF and your agent will read it, answer questions about the contents, summarize it, and even generate interactive graphs based on that document. Now, all this already sounds like a pretty [...] I can also create things like a to-do list app where I can check off tasks where it could be a quiz app, a puzzle game, or even a portfolio website. all within the same interface and using the same models you're already familiar with. Let me show you a few examples of the kinds of agents with artifacts that you can create in Chat LM. Here's a patient engagement planner. First, I can select the clinic services I'm offering, then choose the goals I want to achieve and specify the patients demographic and literacy level. All the input fields are customizable. Some are large text boxes, others are check marks with multiple choices, and some are single choice options. And all of them work together seamlessly to collect the right data. Here's another cool one. Customer support agent that takes [...] spend, you can create some pretty complex agents that will seriously speed up any workflow you have. I'm actually thinking of creating an agent that takes the 50 most popular YouTube videos on a specific topic and gives me a list of ideas with thumbnails, titles, keywords, and even full scripts. But actually, I don't even have to do that because there's already a powerful agent right inside Chat LM. It's called Deep Agent Pro and it's really powerful. It can build apps, write reports, create presentations, and automatically connect to all your systems and perform agentic tasks. With Deep Agent, I can do detailed data analysis and create interactive dashboards in a click with Deep Agent figuring all steps out on its own. Creates apps and I can even set custom domains for hosting these

results.title: AI agents just got their missing piece : interfaces that actually work - Tapptic
results.content:
With MCP Apps, you don’t just “appear in the conversation.” You close the loop inside it : clear comparisons, instant filters, one-click selection, checkout-ready actions without sending the customer elsewhere.

The UI makes decisions effortless. And when decisions take a few clicks, conversion stops being luck it becomes design.

If the interface can’t show options, compare tradeoffs, and let them act, the agent becomes a nice demo not a revenue channel.

## MCP Apps : the missing piece that makes agents actually do things

The core shift is simple :

Before : agents could only talk.

Now : agents can show and collect structured input in context without leaving the conversation.

This is not cosmetic UI. It changes what becomes viable to build. [...] Open the PDF separately. Search for the clauses it mentioned. Copy-paste into notes. Return to chat. Type “approved” or “rejected” for each clause. Repeat 12 times.

That’s not “AI transformation.” That’s unpaid intern work with extra steps.

MCP Apps solves this. OpenAI and Anthropic just standardized interactive UI inside conversational flows, transforming agents from text processors into production-ready platforms.

Here’s what changed and what it means for your business.

## The problem : agents without interfaces don’t scale

Most enterprise agent projects don’t fail because the model is inaccurate. They fail because the human workflow is broken. [...] …all flow back to the agent as clean structured data.

Concrete example : configuring a cloud environment used to be 30 questions. Now it’s one form with dependent fields, validation, smart defaultsand a single submit.

### 3) Sandbox security by design

Everything runs in sandboxed iframes with restricted permissions.

Hosts can review HTML before rendering. All UI-to-host messages are auditable.

This matters because security is the enterprise blocker. MCP Apps isn’t “let the model run arbitrary UI.” It’s “render controlled interfaces with controlled communication.”

## 

## Why standardization accelerates everything

Before standardization, every platform built UI differently.

Build for ChatGPT? Rebuild for Claude. Rebuild again elsewhere.

results.content:
## Instant Support on Your Customers’ Terms

Let customers take the wheel and decide when and how they engage with your service.

Omnipresent

Personalized

Transaction-Ready

Accessible & Inclusive

Cognigy Chat AI Agents

## Omnipresent

Engage with customers on their channel of choice and be available round-the-clock to assist them whenever they need help. 

Cognigy AI Agents are readily accessible on 30+ channels and can be deployed easily on any other preferred touchpoint via standard interfaces.

chat AI Agents (1)-1-1.png?width=900&height=810&name=chat%20AI%20Agents%20(1)-1.png)

## Personalized

Deliver personalized and context-sensitive responses that match conversation history and customer sentiment. [...] By working with your CRM, our AI Agents can immediately identify where customers are in their journey, ensuring consistent experiences everywhere.

Cognigy Chat AI Agents

## Transaction-Ready

From rebooking, cancellation, and order status support to invoice requests, payment, and refunds, AI Agents assist customers in quickly and effectively executing transactions.

Beyond answering questions, they can anticipate customer needs and provide proactive assistance, elevating customer experience and boosting sales.

Cognigy Chat AI Agents.png?width=900&height=810&name=chat%20AI%20Agents%20(1).png)

## Accessible & Inclusive

Ensure the quality of service for everyone and effectively navigate dynamic regulatory landscapes.

There’s a huge opportunity for market-specific customizations, especially for local service businesses that don’t have the time (or interest) to wrangle APIs and complex setups. Tailoring agents to fit industry tone, workflows, and customer expectations is where the real long-term value is. Love what you’re building!
- this actually sounds clutch for small biz owners who hate tech stuff. curious how it handles crazy off-script questions though? been messing with assista AI for multi-step chats and it’s surprisingly solid at that. [...] So consider adding that and I am certain you'll find people interested. If it does what you say, shoot me a DM about a demo. [...] - Sounds pretty badass. It's definitely a hot area, too. AI agent chatbots on social, I am seeing allllll the time now. Tried out a few and have found that most don't provide enough control voice and style. Even if its obviously labeled AI, I would like it to sound casual and talk like a person. That's always a refreshing experience when companies let their support people talk like real, normal humans instead of script readers. I like that your solution is for both sales and support. So it handles both outbound and inbound messages? My company did a ton of investigation for social DM chatbots and we were mostly disappointed, but we ended up settling on a two-part setup.
